text: This agreement imposes unlimited liability on the counterparty for all losses, direct and indirect
The provider may modify terms at any time without prior notice
No party may terminate this agreement for convenience at any time
The client waives all claims for defective performance regardless of cause
Vendor has sole authority to adjust pricing without consent
There is no cap on damages for breach of confidentiality obligations
The service provider is not liable for any third-party claims
Customer must accept all risk of delays absent any contractor fault
The contract allows one-sided indemnification in favor of supplier
The seller is entitled to rescind with no consumer remedy
The buyer must indemnify seller for all losses, even those caused by seller
There is no termination right after first year
The agreement requires customer to pay liquidated damages without limit
Supplier determines service scope unilaterally and changes at will
All warranty obligations are discretionary at supplier sole view
Provider may terminate service for any reason, with no transition
Buyer waives all statutory rights and remedies
No limit is set for penalty interest on late payment
The client must accept ambiguous deliverable descriptions
Vendor can impose hidden fees with no dispute process
There is no mechanism to cap exposure for data breach losses
The contract binds customer to unspecified performance standards
Supplier may assign debts to related parties without approval
Client indemnifies vendor for punitive damages in all cases
No formal notice period is required for contract changes
The provider may block service without justification
One party may terminate only with 180 days notice while other may not
Customer has no right to audit supplier compliance
The agreement requires customer to accept vague service levels
Provider can suspend services for any reason without remedy
The payment obligation continues despite contract termination
The vendor holds all intellectual property rights with no license back
Supplier is not responsible for any subcontractor misconduct
Contract language gives sole discretion to supplier for dispute outcome
No cap on attorney fees in case of legal action
The user is prohibited from terminating even for breach
Supplier can unilaterally extend term indefinitely
All obligations are one-way in favor of the provider
The service provider may revoke access without cause
The client is obligated to indemnify for client and provider losses
No objective criteria for performance are included
The buyer must accept contract modifications unannounced
Supplier may charge arbitrary penalties with no limits
The agreement contains vague confidentiality obligations
One-sided exclusivity clause binds customer to supplier only
No right to terminate if supplier provides nonconforming services
The provider can alter scope and refuse refunds
Claims period is forever with no limitation
This clause mandates unlimited penalty for late delivery
Customer has no right to dispute fees
Parties agree to clear division of responsibilities and defined deliverables
Either party may terminate for material breach with 30 days notice
Liability is limited to direct damages capped at contract value
The agreement includes a termination clause with mutual consent
Both parties share compliance obligations in writing
Supplier must provide services in accordance with agreed quality levels
Customer may audit supplier performance once per quarter
The contract provides a mutual indemnity framework
Damages are capped and limited to reasonable amounts
Both parties retain equal rights to terminate for cause
Responsibilities are clearly documented and allocated
Dispute resolution is balanced and includes mediation
Termination requires 60 days written notice by either party
The clause sets a reasoned liability cap and insurance requirement
All obligations are defined and measurable
Each party has clear and reciprocal confidentiality duties
Contract includes fair termination and transition assistance
Penalties for breach are proportional and predefined
Supplier is liable only for breaches caused by its own conduct
Customer may terminate on vendor material breach with cure period
The agreement defines scope and excludes ambigous terms
Rights and duties are balanced with equal dispute rights
Reasonable limitation of liability is stated explicitly
Both sides approve changes through written amendment
Performance metrics are clear and monitored regularly
Contract offers fair indemnity limits and mutual protection
The vendor must provide 30 days notice before changes
Service levels are quantifiable and enforceable
The agreement includes a mutual termination process
Each party can terminate with a 30-day cure period
Claims are limited to three years from event date
Liability exclusions are not allowed for willful misconduct
Pricing adjustments must be agreed in writing
The contract has a defined scope and balanced responsibilities
Customer can suspend services for nonpayment with notice
Both parties maintain equal rights over data use
Seller commits to delivering according to milestone schedule
Termination triggers equitable wind-down obligations
Supplier must provide regular compliance reports
The clause protects both parties with mutual remedies
Contract is balanced and defines each party’s obligations
Payment terms are clear and include grace period
Force majeure term is mutual and narrowly defined
Renewal is optional and requires written agreement
Data security responsibilities are shared and explicit
Both parties have the right to terminate on insolvency
Party may request written approval for subcontracting
Liability cap is tied to fees paid in last 12 months
Services are defined in precise deliverables and timelines
Notice periods and dispute escalation steps are explicit
